FR-01.title: Account Opening
requirements: Online application for savings and checking accounts
Initial deposit via card or transfer

FR-02.title: Fund Transfer
requirements: Transfer to other bank accounts via routing number

FR-03.title: Bill Payment
requirements: Save frequent billers for quick access

FR-04.title: Transaction History
requirements: View all transactions with date, amount, and type
Filter by date range, type, and amount
Export statement as PDF or CSV

FR-05.title: Two-Factor Authentication
requirements: OTP via SMS or authenticator app for login
OTP required for fund transfers above threshold

FR-06.title: Card Management
requirements: View linked debit and credit cards
Set spending limits per transaction and daily

FR-07.title: Loan Application
requirements: Apply for personal loan with amount and tenure
System calculates EMI based on interest rate

FR-08.title: Account Alerts and Notifications
requirements: Balance alerts for low balance threshold
Transaction alerts for debits above set amount

FR-09.title: Budget and Spending Analytics
requirements: Budget setting per category with alerts

FR-01.title: Plan Account Opening Workflow and Contracts
FR-01.description: Review the SRS requirement bullets for account opening and break them into executable API, UI, data, security, and reporting subtasks. Define the workflow states, user roles, field-level validations, request/response contracts, and failure cases before implementation starts. Use these requirement drivers as the baseline: Online application for savings and checking accounts; KYC document upload and verification; Initial deposit via card or transfer.
acceptance_criteria: The end-to-end workflow for account opening is documented from trigger to completion
Validation rules, role restrictions, and error scenarios for account opening are explicitly defined
Implementation subtasks for account opening can be picked up independently by engineering

FR-01.title: Implement Account Opening Backend Services
FR-01.description: Build the backend services for account opening on React, Java Spring Boot, Oracle DB. Create route handlers or service endpoints, model the core business operations, enforce validation and authorization rules, persist workflow state changes, and add structured error handling and audit logging. Make sure the implementation covers these behaviors: Online application for savings and checking accounts; KYC document upload and verification; Initial deposit via card or transfer.
acceptance_criteria: Core account opening business rules are implemented for Banking Application
Validation, authorization, and error handling are covered for account opening
API/service behavior for account opening is testable and documented for integration

FR-01.title: Implement Account Opening Frontend Flow
FR-01.description: Build the frontend flow for account opening with the exact forms, tables, filters, wizards, status indicators, and action buttons required by the SRS. Connect the UI to the backend contract, handle optimistic updates or refresh flows where needed, and provide user-facing validation, permissions feedback, loading states, and recovery states.
acceptance_criteria: The account opening user flow is accessible and responsive across supported browsers
Forms, filters, tables, and feedback states for account opening behave correctly
The account opening UI is fully integrated with backend APIs and handles loading and error states

FR-01.title: Design Account Opening Persistence Model
FR-01.description: Design the persistence layer for account opening. Identify the core entities, relationships, indexes, status fields, history/audit tables, and reporting attributes required to support the workflow. Add migrations and seed or fixture data where that helps engineering validate the feature early.
acceptance_criteria: The persistence model for account opening supports required workflows and reporting needs
Indexes, constraints, and relationships for account opening are defined correctly
Migrations for account opening can be applied safely in target environments

FR-01.title: Write Account Opening Automated and End-to-End Tests
FR-01.description: Write unit, integration, and end-to-end tests for account opening. Cover valid flows, invalid inputs, permission failures, state transitions, data integrity checks, and regression-prone edge cases pulled from the SRS. Add fixtures or mocks that make the feature verifiable in CI and local development.
acceptance_criteria: Happy path and failure cases for account opening are covered by automated tests
Permission checks and validation rules for account opening are verified
Regression coverage exists for the most critical account opening scenarios

FR-10.title: Implement Customer Support Backend Services
FR-10.description: Build the backend services for customer support on React, Java Spring Boot, Oracle DB. Create route handlers or service endpoints, model the core business operations, enforce validation and authorization rules, persist workflow state changes, and add structured error handling and audit logging. Make sure the implementation covers these behaviors: In-app chat with support agents; Call back request feature; FAQ and help articles section.
acceptance_criteria: Core customer support business rules are implemented for Banking Application
Validation, authorization, and error handling are covered for customer support
API/service behavior for customer support is testable and documented for integration

FR-10.title: Implement Customer Support Frontend Flow
FR-10.description: Build the frontend flow for customer support with the exact forms, tables, filters, wizards, status indicators, and action buttons required by the SRS. Connect the UI to the backend contract, handle optimistic updates or refresh flows where needed, and provide user-facing validation, permissions feedback, loading states, and recovery states.
acceptance_criteria: The customer support user flow is accessible and responsive across supported browsers
Forms, filters, tables, and feedback states for customer support behave correctly
The customer support UI is fully integrated with backend APIs and handles loading and error states

FR-10.title: Design Customer Support Persistence Model
FR-10.description: Design the persistence layer for customer support. Identify the core entities, relationships, indexes, status fields, history/audit tables, and reporting attributes required to support the workflow. Add migrations and seed or fixture data where that helps engineering validate the feature early.
acceptance_criteria: The persistence model for customer support supports required workflows and reporting needs
Indexes, constraints, and relationships for customer support are defined correctly
Migrations for customer support can be applied safely in target environments

FR-10.title: Write Customer Support Automated and End-to-End Tests
FR-10.description: Write unit, integration, and end-to-end tests for customer support. Cover valid flows, invalid inputs, permission failures, state transitions, data integrity checks, and regression-prone edge cases pulled from the SRS. Add fixtures or mocks that make the feature verifiable in CI and local development.
acceptance_criteria: Happy path and failure cases for customer support are covered by automated tests
Permission checks and validation rules for customer support are verified
Regression coverage exists for the most critical customer support scenarios
